hooks.command: [ -f .understand-anything-ssot/knowledge-graph.json ] && printf '%s\n' '<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT> This repo runs the dual knowledge-graph workflow. STANDING RULE for this entire session: before ANY substantive code or architecture work, your FIRST action MUST be to invoke the kg-context-dispatch agent via the Task tool, and you must let IT decide whether to skip. It loads SSOT intent first (status, acceptance, contract, touch_budget), then Impl reality and blast radius, plus prior decisions, and returns a side-effect warning. Do not edit files or commit to a design before it returns. Consult SSOT first, Impl second. Respect touch_budget. Derive tests from acceptance. This is not optional and you cannot rationalize your way out of it. </EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>' || true

if [ -f .understand-anything-ssot/knowledge-graph.json ] && [ -f .understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json ]; then if [ -f engine/scripts/ssot_diff.py ]; then python3 engine/scripts/ssot_diff.py --quiet 2>/dev/null; elif [ -f scripts/ssot_diff.py ]; then python3 scripts/ssot_diff.py --quiet 2>/dev/null; fi; fi; true

[ -f .understand-anything-ssot/knowledge-graph.json ] && printf '%s\n' '<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT> kg-workflow: before doing anything substantive on THIS prompt, your FIRST tool call MUST be the kg-context-dispatch agent (Task tool). ALWAYS invoke it first and let the dispatcher itself decide whether to skip; it self-skips cheaply for greetings, meta questions, and trivial follow-ups, and otherwise returns SSOT intent (status, acceptance, contract, touch_budget), Impl blast radius, and a side-effect warning. Do not edit files, write code, or design until it returns. Consult SSOT first, Impl second. Respect touch_budget. This is not optional and you cannot rationalize your way out of it. </EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>' || true

sh -c 'IN=$(cat 2>/dev/null); printf "%s" "$IN" | grep -qE "git[[:space:]]+(commit|merge|cherry-pick|rebase)" || exit 0; [ -f .understand-anything-ssot/knowledge-graph.json ] || exit 0; printf "%s\n" "<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT> kg-workflow: a commit/merge just happened. Track this change in BOTH knowledge graphs now, without asking the user: (1) Impl KG — if .understand-anything/config.json has autoUpdate true, ensure the understand-anything incremental update runs so the graph matches the new code. (2) SSOT KG — if this commit changed intent, behavior, or a contract, append a Decision Log entry under docs/ssot/decisions/ and run the ssot replay script (engine/scripts/ssot_replay.py or scripts/ssot_replay.py) so the SSOT graph tracks it. Then stage and commit the updated graph files. </EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>"'
